summary: Reviewed AnatomyGlobeVisual.tsx, AmbientEditorialHero.tsx, BrandMark.tsx, globals.css, frontpages 67–76, and logo options 451–500. Globe line presence is still insufficient in both the live component and the SVG comps — the sphere outer circle is the only 'main' weight element (opacity 0.2), while orbit ellipses and 3-of-4 latitude lines render at 0.08–0.12 opacity and effectively vanish. The right side is filled by one dominant medical object (torso+chest-ellipse+teal spine accent), which is correct, but the figure trails off below the waist with no anchoring weight. The globe does NOT yet feel as legible as Anthropic's front-page clarity — it reads as a faint background texture supporting the figure, rather than as a co-equal globe-plus-figure composition. The BrandMark.tsx glyph (stacked arcs + vertical axis in a pill icon) is coherent with the system palette but the axis rendering depends on CSS classes (site-mark-glyph-axis, -arch, -basin, -ring) that ARE defined in globals.css — that part is safe. Critical flag: AnatomyGlobeVisual uses class-based strokes (hero-globe-shell-line, hero-globe-orbit-line, etc.) that do NOT appear in the first 300 lines of globals.css; if they are absent further down, the globe renders completely unstyled.
recommendations: Search globals.css below line 300 for hero-globe-shell-line, hero-globe-orbit-line, hero-globe-axis-line, hero-globe-latitude-line, hero-globe-meridian-line, hero-globe-focus-core, hero-globe-focus-shell, hero-globe-perimeter-dots, hero-anatomy-* classes. If absent, add them. Minimum viable: shell-line at stroke rgba(118,108,93,0.26) stroke-width 1.5; orbit-line at rgba(93,133,144,0.22) 1.2; axis-line at rgba(194,154,86,0.9) 2.0; latitude-line at rgba(118,108,93,0.22) 1.3; meridian-line at rgba(118,108,93,0.16) 1.1; anatomy-heartline at rgba(47,42,37,0.38) 2.0; anatomy-spine-line at rgba(93,133,144,0.9) 2.2. Without these the globe is invisible.

In AnatomyGlobeVisual.tsx the hero-globe-shell-line class needs stroke-width ≥ 1.6 and opacity ≥ 0.26. In frontpage SVGs 67–76, bump .shell.main from rgba(122,113,99,0.2) → rgba(122,113,99,0.28) and stroke-width 1.46 → 1.64. Add 1 additional meridian path to the globe group so the sphere reads as spherical, not just circular. The current 3 meridians in AnatomyGlobeVisual are good but the frontpage SVGs only carry 1 effective meridian — they need a second at roughly 30° offset from the first.

Promote FP67, FP71, FP73 as the 3 strongest. FP67: best headline + large globe + classic sweep figure. FP71: cleanest conceptual clarity (names the visual object), large globe, symmetric torso. FP73: most direct product claim + explicitly cues globe lines in body copy. Archive FP68, 69, 70, 72, 74, 75, 76. For logos: retire the Globe+Serif-I family (465, 470 and any variant) — the I letterform reads as pharmacy-brand-2008 and is visually ambiguous. Retire the Pill/EKG-waveform family (475 and variants) — zero premium signal, generic hospital brandmark. Keep investigation alive only on Globe+Figure icon family (451, 455, 460), Droplet/Shield family (485), and Arch/Wordmark editorial family (500).

451: Globe+figure, terra axis, teal orbit ellipse, chest ellipse + shoulder/basin arcs — most coherent with hero visual language, carry forward. 460: Same family as 451 with teal axis swap — works in dark contexts, carry forward. 485: Droplet/shield silhouette with teal spine + chest ellipse + terra horizontal bar — most distinctive small-size silhouette, carry forward. 500: Arch wordmark (two overlapping arch strokes above Source Serif 4 ChapAI) — most editorial/premium, unique in the batch, carry forward. 455: Globe+figure slight offset variant vs 451 — secondary candidate, hold for creator A/B. Next cut: pick 1 of 451 vs 455 before outreach (451 has terra axis which ties more directly to the terra brand token; prefer 451 unless creator preference is teal-dominant).

durable_memory: AnatomyGlobeVisual.tsx uses CSS class-based strokes; verify these classes exist in globals.css below line 300 before assuming globe is rendering correctly
Frontpage SVGs 67-76 all share identical anatomy figure paths and globe shell structure; variation is headline copy and globe radius (r=430 large vs r=392 smaller)

Two distinct anatomy figure variants exist across FP67-76: Variant A (classic sweep — figure extends left, organ arcs right) used in FP67/73; Variant B (symmetric torso — bilateral curves) used in FP68/71
Brand token mapping: terra=#C29A56 / #C39A61, teal=#5D8590, dark=#1F2024, muted=#74685A, bg=#F4ECDE, surface=#FBF7F0
active_context: Globe premium reset review complete — lines present in SVG paths but CSS class definitions unconfirmed below globals.css line 300
Three strongest frontpages for creator outreach: FP67, FP71, FP73
Pre-outreach gate: R1 (CSS class audit) must be resolved before showing live product to creators

current_blocker: AnatomyGlobeVisual.tsx CSS class strokes (hero-globe-shell-line and all hero-anatomy-* variants) not visible in globals.css lines 1-300. If undefined further down, the entire globe renders as blank paths on the live site. This is a ship-blocker for creator outreach — they will open the URL and see a broken or empty right column.
suggested_human_fix: Run a grep for 'hero-globe-shell-line' in globals.css. If no match: copy the inline style block from any frontpage SVG (e.g. frontpage-67.svg lines 10-18), translate the .shell/.figure/.organ/.accent/.detail class values into the corresponding hero-globe-* and hero-anatomy-* CSS class definitions in globals.css under a new @layer components block, then hot-reload and verify the globe is visible before any creator link is shared.
next_task: Audit globals.css for all hero-globe-* and hero-anatomy-* class definitions (R1), then bump .shell.main opacity and add one meridian line to the frontpage SVGs (R2). Both can be done in one session under 75 minutes. After that the 3-concept frontpage slate (FP67, FP71, FP73) and top-5 logo list (451, 460, 485, 500, 455) are ready for creator DM outreach.
